careers.title: Digital Marketing Manager
careers.description: A Digital Marketing Manager develops and executes online marketing strategies across channels including search engine optimisation (SEO), search engine marketing (SEM/PPC), social media marketing, email marketing, content marketing, affiliate marketing, and paid advertising. In India's rapidly expanding digital economy — with 800+ million internet users and one of the world's highest mobile data consumption rates — Digital Marketing Managers are employed by e-commerce companies, FMCG brands, banks, startups, and digital agencies. They use tools like Google Analytics, SEMrush, HubSpot, Meta Ads Manager, and Google Ads to measure and optimise campaign performance.
personality_traits: Data-driven analytical mindset
Creative content and campaign strategy
Proficiency with digital marketing tools
Cross-channel marketing integration
Understanding of SEO, SEM, and paid media
Communication and client management
Continuous learning about platform changes
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (Commerce or Science preferred for analytical skills); MBA Marketing or Digital Marketing specialisation is the standard professional pathway
entrance_exams: MICAT (MICA Ahmedabad — premier institute for advertising and marketing communications)
CAT / XAT (for MBA Marketing at IIM/XLRI)
CUET (for BBA Marketing / BA Mass Communication entry)
No dedicated national digital marketing exam; Google Ads Certification, Meta Blueprint, HubSpot Inbound Marketing Certification are industry-recognised
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. BBA/B.Com/BA Mass Communication (Marketing electives).
3. MBA Marketing / PGDM Communications (MICA, IIM, NMIMS).
4. Google Ads and Google Analytics Certification (free).
5. Meta Blueprint Certification (free).
6. HubSpot Content Marketing and Inbound Certification (free).
7. Entry: Digital Marketing Executive → Digital Marketing Manager → Head of Digital Marketing → CMO.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: BBA/BA Mass Communication (Govt): INR 20,000–1,00,000/yr; MBA Marketing (MICA): INR 10,00,000–15,00,000 total; MBA Marketing Private: INR 2,00,000–8,00,000; Google Ads Certification: free; Meta Blueprint: free; Online digital marketing bootcamps: INR 10,000–1,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: Digital Marketing Executive → Senior Executive → Digital Marketing Manager → Senior Manager → Head of Digital Marketing → VP of Marketing → Chief Marketing Officer (CMO)

work_environment.places_of_work: E-commerce companies (Amazon India, Flipkart, Nykaa, Meesho), FMCG brands (HUL, ITC, P&G India), banks, startups (Bengaluru, Mumbai, Gurugram), digital marketing agencies (Dentsu Digital India, Schbang, FoxyMoron, Kinnect).
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; data-intensive screen work managing multiple channels simultaneously. Regular campaign reporting and optimisation. Collaborative with content, design, and product teams. Fast-changing digital landscape requiring continuous learning.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own digital marketing agency or independent consulting practice.

careers.title: Sales Director
careers.description: A Sales Director (also called Head of Sales) is a senior executive who leads the entire sales organisation of a company — setting strategy, managing sales managers and teams, building client relationships at the C-suite level, and driving revenue growth. In India, Sales Directors are employed across all commercial sectors — FMCG, SaaS, IT services, banking, real estate, pharma, and manufacturing. They are responsible for sales forecasting, team structure, incentive design, key account management, and alignment with marketing, product, and finance teams. India's large and competitive business environment makes Sales Directors among the most strategically important leaders in any company.
personality_traits: Strategic sales planning and execution
Leadership and team development
Relationship management at C-suite level
Revenue forecasting and pipeline management
Negotiation and deal closing at senior level
Data-driven performance management
Cross-functional collaboration with marketing and product
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Commerce or Science preferred; MBA Sales/Marketing is the standard pathway to Sales Director
entrance_exams: CAT / XAT / GMAT (for MBA Sales/Marketing — IIM, ISB, XLRI)
MICAT (MICA Ahmedabad)
CUET (for BBA entry)
No dedicated national Sales Director exam; Salesforce, HubSpot, and Miller Heiman certifications are valued
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. Graduate in BBA/B.Com/B.Tech (any discipline).
3. MBA in Sales/Marketing (IIM, ISB, XLRI) is strongly preferred for Director level roles.
4. Build 10–15 years of progressive sales experience: Salesperson → Manager → Senior Manager → Sales Director.
5. Gain expertise in CRM tools, sales analytics, and revenue operations (RevOps).
6. Certifications: SPIN Selling, Miller Heiman Strategic Selling, Challenger Sale training.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: Graduation (any): INR 10,000–80,000/yr Govt; MBA Sales/Marketing (IIM/ISB): INR 10,00,000–23,00,000; Sales training programmes (Miller Heiman, Challenger Sale): INR 20,000–80,000; Executive MBA (IIM/ISB): INR 15,00,000–30,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: Sales Executive → Senior Sales Executive → Area Sales Manager → Regional Sales Manager → National Sales Head → Sales Director → VP of Sales → Chief Revenue Officer (CRO)

work_environment.places_of_work: FMCG companies (HUL, ITC, P&G, Nestlé India), SaaS companies, IT services, banking and financial services, pharmaceutical companies, real estate developers, manufacturing conglomerates.
work_environment.work_environment: Senior executive environment; mix of client meetings at CXO level, internal team reviews, board presentations, and revenue planning. High travel (domestic and international for large companies). High-pressure, high-reward role.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can leverage sales leadership network to start own business or sales consulting firm.

careers.title: Administrative Assistant
careers.description: An Administrative Assistant (also Executive Assistant or Administrative Coordinator) provides administrative support to managers, executives, or departments by managing calendars, scheduling meetings, handling correspondence, maintaining files, coordinating travel arrangements, and preparing reports and presentations. In India, Administrative Assistants are employed across all industries in corporate offices, government departments, embassies, hospitals, educational institutions, and NGOs. Executive Assistants who support C-suite executives (CEOs, CFOs, CMDs) are a specialised senior variant that requires strong communication, discretion, and multi-tasking ability at a high-stakes level.
personality_traits: Organisational and multi-tasking ability
Professional communication (written and verbal)
Discretion and confidentiality
Computer proficiency (MS Office, Google Workspace)
Attention to scheduling and deadline detail
Adaptability and problem-solving
Professional demeanour
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Class 10+2 pass minimum; Commerce or Arts/Humanities preferred; graduation preferred for corporate roles
entrance_exams: SSC CHSL (Combined Higher Secondary Level) — for government administrative assistant posts
SSC MTS (for entry-level administrative support in government)
State government administrative service exams
IBPS Clerk / SBI Clerk (for bank administrative roles)
No dedicated private-sector national exam; employer-level aptitude and communication test
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. Graduate in any discipline (BBA/BA/B.Com preferred for corporate roles).
3. Develop proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), Google Workspace, and scheduling tools.
4. Optional: Diploma in Office Management and Secretarial Practice (NIIT, IGNOU, private institutes).
5. NIELIT CCC certification — recommended for government roles.
6. Entry: Office Assistant → Administrative Assistant → Senior Administrative Assistant → Executive Assistant.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: Class 10+2 (Govt school): INR 0–10,000 total; BBA/BA (Govt): INR 20,000–80,000/yr; SSC CHSL coaching: INR 5,000–30,000; NIELIT CCC: INR 590; Diploma in Office Management: INR 10,000–50,000
careers.career_growth_path: Office Assistant → Administrative Assistant → Senior Administrative Assistant → Executive Assistant → Office Manager → Administration Manager → Head of Administration

work_environment.places_of_work: Corporate offices across all industries, government departments (central and state), embassies and international organisations, hospitals, educational institutions, NGOs, startups.
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based; desk work involving scheduling, correspondence, filing, and coordination. Professional, structured environment. Discretion essential in executive-level roles. Regular office hours generally.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own virtual assistant or administrative services business for SMEs.

careers.title: IT Support Analyst
careers.description: An IT Support Analyst (also Help Desk Analyst or Technical Support Specialist) provides technical assistance and support for computer hardware, software, networks, and IT systems to end users within an organisation or for external clients. In India, IT Support Analysts are employed in IT departments of all large companies, BPO/KPO firms (providing offshore support to global clients), banks, hospitals, government agencies, and IT service providers. India is the world's largest hub for IT helpdesk and technical support outsourcing. Support Analysts resolve issues via ticketing systems (ServiceNow, Freshdesk, Jira), remote desktop tools, and on-site visits.
personality_traits: Technical problem-solving and diagnostic aptitude
Patience and customer service orientation
Communication clarity (written and verbal)
Continuous learning about technology
Attention to service ticket detail
Teamwork with IT teams
Documentation discipline
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) or Any stream; B.Tech CS/IT or BCA is the standard pathway; ITI COPA for vocational entry
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CS/IT — formal engineering pathway)
State engineering entrance exams
No dedicated national IT Support exam; CompTIA A+ is the globally recognised entry certification; ITIL Foundation for process knowledge
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream (Science/Mathematics preferred).
2. B.Tech CS/IT OR BCA OR B.Sc CS (formal route); OR ITI COPA (vocational route).
3. Obtain CompTIA A+ certification (entry-level hardware and software troubleshooting — globally valued in India).
4. Learn ITIL Foundation (IT Infrastructure Library — service management standard).
5. Learn ticketing and remote support tools: ServiceNow, Freshdesk, Jira, AnyDesk, TeamViewer.
6. Entry: L1 Support Executive → L2 Support Analyst → Senior IT Support Analyst → IT Manager.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: BCA (Govt): INR 20,000–80,000/yr; B.Tech CS/IT (Govt): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; CompTIA A+ certification: INR 10,000–20,000 (USD 239 per exam); ITIL Foundation: INR 8,000–15,000; ITI COPA: INR 15,000–40,000/yr
careers.career_growth_path: L1 Support Executive → L2 IT Support Analyst → Senior IT Support Analyst → IT Team Lead → IT Manager → IT Director → Chief Information Officer (CIO)

work_environment.places_of_work: IT departments of large companies, BPO/KPO firms providing offshore IT support (Concentrix, WiPro BPS, EXL, Tech Mahindra), banks, hospitals, government agencies, IT managed services providers (MSPs).
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; desk-based work on computer with phone/chat interactions. Ticket-driven environment with SLA (Service Level Agreement) targets. Shift work common for 24/7 global support coverage.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own IT support and managed services company serving SMEs.

careers.title: Legal Counsel
careers.description: A Legal Counsel (also In-House Lawyer or Corporate Legal Counsel) is a lawyer employed directly by a company or organisation — rather than at a law firm — to provide legal advice, manage legal risk, draft and negotiate contracts, ensure regulatory compliance, and handle disputes. In India, large corporations (Tata, Reliance, Infosys, HDFC, HUL), technology companies, banks, FMCG firms, and startups employ in-house Legal Counsel teams. With the Companies Act 2013, GST, SEBI regulations, data protection laws, and expanding merger control regulations, the role of In-House Legal Counsel has become more strategic and valued in Indian corporate governance.
personality_traits: Analytical legal reasoning
Attention to statutory and contract detail
Business acumen and commercial judgment
Communication with senior management and regulators
Risk assessment and mitigation
Negotiation skills
Ethical integrity and confidentiality
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Law degree (BA LLB / BBA LLB) is mandatory; additional technical or business degree is valued for specific sectors
entrance_exams: CLAT (Common Law Admission Test — for 5-year BA LLB / BBA LLB at National Law Universities)
AILET (All India Law Entrance Test — NLU Delhi)
State law school entrance exams (LSAT India, MHT-CET Law)
Bar Council of India enrolment (mandatory for all advocates in India)
LLM entrance exams (NLU Delhi, Symbiosis, NALSAR) for specialisation
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. 5-year integrated BA LLB / BBA LLB at National Law University (NLU) OR 3-year LLB after graduation.
3. Enrol with State Bar Council (mandatory for legal practice in India).
4. Start career at a law firm (AZB, Khaitan, Trilegal) to build corporate legal skills.
5. Transition to in-house counsel role at a company after 3–5 years of law firm experience.
6. LLM in Corporate/Commercial Law, IP, Tax, or Competition Law for specialisation.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: 5-year BA LLB (Govt NLU): INR 50,000–3,00,000/yr; 3-year LLB (Govt law college): INR 10,000–80,000/yr; LLB Private law schools: INR 1,00,000–5,00,000/yr; LLM (NLU Delhi, Symbiosis): INR 50,000–3,00,000/yr; Bar Council enrolment: INR 500–10,000
careers.career_growth_path: Legal Intern → Junior Associate (Law Firm) → Associate → Senior Associate → In-House Counsel / Legal Counsel → Senior Legal Counsel → Head of Legal → General Counsel / Chief Legal Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: Corporate in-house legal departments (Tata, Reliance, Infosys, HUL, HDFC), law firms (AZB & Partners, Khaitan & Co, Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas, Trilegal), technology and fintech companies, SEBI/RBI-regulated entities.
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based; mix of contract review, legal research, and meetings with business teams and external counsel. Requires strict confidentiality. High-pressure during M&A, IPO, or regulatory investigations.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own legal consultancy or boutique law firm.

careers.title: Network Engineer
careers.description: A Network Engineer designs, implements, manages, and troubleshoots computer networks — including LAN, WAN, wireless, cloud, and SD-WAN infrastructure — for organisations. In India, Network Engineers are employed by IT services companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL), telecom operators (Jio, Airtel, BSNL, Vodafone Idea), banks, government agencies, data centres, and IT managed services providers (MSPs). India's massive digital infrastructure expansion — including the rollout of 5G networks, National Broadband Mission, BharatNet, and Smart Cities — creates sustained demand for skilled network professionals. Cisco certifications (CCNA, CCNP, CCIE) are highly valued by Indian employers.
personality_traits: Analytical network troubleshooting aptitude
Technical knowledge of routing, switching, and security
Attention to network architecture and performance detail
Problem-solving under operational pressure
Communication with IT teams and management
Safety and security consciousness
Continuous learning about networking technologies
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech ECE/CS/IT or BCA is the standard pathway; Cisco and CompTIA certifications are often valued over formal degrees
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech ECE/CS/IT — formal engineering pathway)
GATE (ECE/CS for M.Tech)
State engineering entrance exams (MHT-CET, KCET, WBJEE)
CCNA (Cisco Certified Network Associate) exam — most valued industry certification for network engineers in India
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Mathematics essential).
2. B.Tech ECE/CS/IT OR BCA OR B.Sc CS (formal route).
3. Obtain CCNA certification (Cisco Certified Network Associate) — most valued networking certification globally and in India.
4. Progress: CCNP Enterprise / Security / Data Centre for senior roles.
5. Learn cloud networking: AWS Networking Specialty, Azure Network Engineer Associate.
6. Entry: Network Technician → Junior Network Engineer → Network Engineer → Senior Network Engineer → Network Architect.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech ECE/CS/IT (Govt NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; BCA (Govt): INR 20,000–80,000/yr; CCNA exam: INR 20,000–30,000 (USD 330); CCNP exam: INR 30,000–40,000 per exam; CompTIA Network+: INR 15,000–25,000
careers.career_growth_path: Network Technician → Junior Network Engineer → Network Engineer → Senior Network Engineer → Network Lead → Network Architect → Head of IT Infrastructure → CTO

work_environment.places_of_work: IT services companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL), telecom operators (Jio, Airtel, BSNL), banks, data centres, government digital infrastructure agencies (NIC, BSNL), IT managed services providers (MSPs).
work_environment.work_environment: Office and data centre environment; mix of remote monitoring and on-site network maintenance. On-call availability for network outages. Technical, problem-solving, team-based work.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own IT networking and infrastructure services company.

careers.title: Corporate Strategy Manager
careers.description: A Corporate Strategy Manager develops and executes long-term strategic plans for a company, identifies growth opportunities (organic and inorganic), conducts competitive analysis, evaluates M&A targets, and advises senior leadership on strategic priorities. In India, Corporate Strategy Managers are employed at the strategy teams of large corporations (Tata Strategic Management Group, Mahindra Group Strategy, Reliance Industries Strategy), consulting firms (McKinsey, BCG, Bain, Kearney, Deloitte India), private equity firms, and investment banks. The role is a coveted post-MBA career path for IIM and ISB graduates entering consulting or in-house strategy.
personality_traits: Strategic and systems thinking
Analytical and quantitative problem-solving
Financial modelling and valuation
Storytelling and executive communication
Market research and competitive intelligence
Stakeholder management
Intellectual curiosity and creativity
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; MBA from a top institution (IIM, ISB, Harvard, Wharton) is typically required for Corporate Strategy roles at large corporations
entrance_exams: CAT / XAT / GMAT (for MBA — the primary pathway to corporate strategy)
JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech — common background for strategy at tech companies)
No dedicated national strategy exam; CFA and PMP are complementary certifications
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. B.Tech/B.E. (any discipline) OR B.Com/BBA (Commerce pathway).
3. MBA from IIM/ISB/XLRI or international business school (Wharton, INSEAD, Harvard, LBS) — most Corporate Strategy roles require an MBA.
4. Entry: Management Consultant at Big 4 / MBB (McKinsey, BCG, Bain) → Corporate Strategy Manager.
5. Key skills: Excel financial modelling, PowerPoint, Python (for advanced analytics), competitive intelligence platforms (Bloomberg, PitchBook, CapIQ).
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: MBA (IIM): INR 10,00,000–23,00,000 total; MBA (ISB): INR 12,00,000–15,00,000; International MBA (Wharton/INSEAD): INR 50,00,000–80,00,000; Executive MBA: INR 15,00,000–30,00,000; B.Tech (IIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr
careers.career_growth_path: Management Consultant / Strategy Analyst → Strategy Manager → Senior Strategy Manager → Director of Strategy → VP of Corporate Strategy → Chief Strategy Officer (CSO) / CEO

work_environment.places_of_work: Corporate strategy teams at large Indian and multinational corporations (Tata, Reliance, Mahindra, HUL, ITC), management consulting firms (McKinsey, BCG, Bain, Kearney, Deloitte S&O, EY-Parthenon, KPMG Strategy), private equity firms, investment banks.
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based; primarily analytical and presentation work. Regular client or internal leadership meetings. Travel to client offices. Fast-paced, intellectually demanding environment. MBA-heavy peer culture.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can transition to startup founding or venture capital after building strategy skills.

careers.title: Ramp Agent (Ground Handling Crew)
careers.description: A Ramp Agent (also called Ground Handling Crew or Aircraft Ground Handling Agent) performs physical tasks on the airport airside to service aircraft between flights. Duties include loading and unloading baggage and cargo, marshalling aircraft to gates, operating ground support equipment (baggage tugs, belt loaders, aircraft stairs), refuelling assistance, cabin cleaning, and securing aircraft for pushback. In India, Ramp Agents are employed by ground handling companies (AISATS, Bird WorldWide Flight Services, Celebi Ground Handling, SATS-Menzies) and airlines at major airports (IGI Delhi, CSIA Mumbai, KIA Bengaluru, RGI Hyderabad, MAA Chennai) which together handle 350+ million passengers annually. Note: This career originated from the Portuguese title 'CGH — Auxiliar Líder de Rampa' (Ramp Lead/Ground Crew Leader) used in Brazil; the role is globally standardised under IATA Airport Handling Manual (AHM) guidelines.
personality_traits: Physical fitness and stamina
Safety consciousness on airside
Team coordination in high-pressure environment
Alertness for FOD (Foreign Object Debris) prevention
Ability to work in all weather conditions
Reliability and punctuality
Communication within ground teams
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Class 10+2 pass minimum; physical fitness mandatory; no formal stream requirement
entrance_exams: No formal national entrance exam
Airline and ground handler company-level recruitment test (physical fitness, medical exam, basic English, aptitude)
IATA Airport Ground Handling (AHM 810) certification — required for airside operations
Airside Safety training certification — mandatory per DGCA regulations
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete Class 10+2 in any stream.
2. Pass medical fitness examination (vision, physical stamina).
3. Obtain Airport Entry Permit (BCAS clearance — Bureau of Civil Aviation Security) — mandatory for airside access in India.
4. Complete IATA Airport Ground Handling course (1–2 weeks) or company-sponsored training.
5. Complete Dangerous Goods (DG) Awareness training (IATA — mandatory for cargo ramp agents).
6. Entry: Ground Handling Agent → Senior Ramp Agent → Ramp Supervisor → Duty Manager (Ground Operations).
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: Ground Handling Company training: INR 5,000–30,000 for IATA-accredited ramp handling certificate; BCAS clearance: minimal fee; Company-sponsored training for direct hires: typically free
careers.career_growth_path: Ground Handling Agent / Ramp Agent → Senior Ramp Agent → Ramp Supervisor → Shift Supervisor → Duty Manager Ground Operations → Ground Operations Manager → Head of Ground Handling

work_environment.places_of_work: Airport airside areas at major Indian airports (IGI Delhi, CSIA Mumbai, KIA Bengaluru, RGI Hyderabad, MAA Chennai), ground handling companies (AISATS, Bird WorldWide, Celebi Ground Handling, SATS-Menzies), airline ground operations departments.
work_environment.work_environment: Outdoor airside environment; physically demanding shift work including nights, early mornings, and weekends. Noise exposure from aircraft engines. All weather conditions. Strict safety protocols and PPE (high-visibility vest, ear protection, safety boots) mandatory.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can leverage airport operations knowledge to start own ground handling consultancy or airport services company.

careers.title: Brand Designer
careers.description: A Brand Designer creates visual identities for companies, products, and organisations — including logos, colour palettes, typography, brand guidelines, packaging, and all visual touchpoints that form a brand's personality. Unlike a General Graphic Designer who works on diverse visual tasks, a Brand Designer specialises in building cohesive, strategic brand identities that communicate a company's values and differentiate it in the market. In India's booming startup ecosystem and consumer brand market — where hundreds of D2C brands (Mamaearth, boAt, Licious, Boat) launch annually — Brand Designers are in high demand at branding agencies, product companies, and in-house design teams.
personality_traits: Visual creativity and aesthetic sensibility
Typography and colour theory expertise
Brand strategy and storytelling understanding
Technical proficiency in design tools (Adobe, Figma)
Client communication and brief interpretation
Attention to brand consistency and guidelines
Market and consumer trend awareness
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (Fine Arts or Science for design programmes); NID DAT and NIFT entrance are the primary professional pathways
entrance_exams: NID DAT (National Institute of Design Design Aptitude Test — B.Des entry)
UCEED (Undergraduate Common Entrance Exam for Design — IIT design programmes)
NIFT entrance exam (for Fashion / Communication Design)
Pearl Academy entrance test
College-specific design school admissions tests
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. B.Des in Communication Design / Visual Communication (NID, NIFT, Pearl Academy, IIT B.Des) OR B.Sc/BA Fine Arts.
3. Build strong portfolio: minimum 15–20 original brand identity projects.
4. Learn design tools: Adobe Illustrator (essential for brand design), Photoshop, Figma, InVision.
5. Study typography: develop deep knowledge of typefaces and their psychological impact.
6. Entry: Junior Brand Designer → Brand Designer → Senior Brand Designer → Brand Identity Director.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Des Communication Design (NID — Govt): INR 40,000–2,00,000/yr; B.Des (NIFT — Govt): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; B.Des (Private Pearl Academy): INR 80,000–5,00,000/yr; Short branding courses (online): INR 5,000–50,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Brand Designer → Brand Designer → Senior Brand Designer → Lead Brand Designer → Brand Identity Director → Head of Design → Chief Design Officer (CDO)

work_environment.places_of_work: Branding agencies (Landor & Fitch India, Elephant Design, Elephant Brand, Elephant Design Pune), digital marketing agencies, D2C startups, FMCG companies (HUL, ITC), tech companies' in-house design teams.
work_environment.work_environment: Design studio or office-based; creative, collaborative, and visually stimulating environment. Mix of solo design work and client presentation. Tight creative brief deadlines. Feedback-iterative work culture.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own branding studio or freelance for clients globally.

careers.title: Medical Assistant
careers.description: A Medical Assistant (MA) performs both administrative and basic clinical tasks in medical offices, clinics, and hospitals — scheduling appointments, managing patient records, billing, taking vital signs, preparing patients for examinations, assisting physicians during procedures, and collecting specimens for laboratory testing. In India, this role overlaps with 'Hospital Ward Assistant', 'General Duty Assistant (GDA)', and 'Multipurpose Healthcare Worker'. The formal title 'Medical Assistant' is more widely used in the USA, UK, and Australia — in India it is increasingly used in private hospitals, multispecialty clinics, corporate healthcare chains (Apollo, Fortis, Manipal), and telemedicine platforms that follow international care delivery models.
personality_traits: Empathy and patient-centred communication
Clinical accuracy and attention to patient safety
Administrative and organisational ability
Medical terminology knowledge
Physical fitness and stamina
Teamwork with doctors and nurses
Confidentiality with patient data
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Biology — all recommended); Class 10+2 pass minimum for vocational entry
entrance_exams: AIIMS Allied Health Sciences entrance (for formal B.Sc pathway)
JIPMER Paramedical entrance
State paramedical board entrance exams
No dedicated national Medical Assistant exam; NSDC / HSSC (Healthcare Sector Skill Council) Medical Assistant certification is nationally recognised in India
NCHMCT JEE (for hospital administration route)
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete Class 10+2 (Science with Biology recommended).
2. Diploma or Certificate in Medical Assistance / General Duty Assistance (GDA) — NSDC / HSSC approved, 3–6 months.
3. OR B.Sc in Medical Laboratory Technology / Allied Health Sciences (formal degree, 3 years).
4. Obtain Basic Life Support (BLS) / First Aid certification.
5. Entry: GDA / Trainee Medical Assistant → Medical Assistant → Senior Medical Assistant → Ward Supervisor.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: NSDC/HSSC GDA Certificate: INR 5,000–20,000 (often free under PMKVY); B.Sc Allied Health Sciences (Govt): INR 20,000–1,00,000/yr; B.Sc Private: INR 50,000–3,00,000/yr; BLS/First Aid certification: INR 2,000–5,000
careers.career_growth_path: Medical Assistant Trainee → Medical Assistant → Senior Medical Assistant → Ward Supervisor → Department Coordinator → Hospital Operations Manager

work_environment.places_of_work: Private hospitals and multispecialty clinics (Apollo, Fortis, Manipal, Max Healthcare), corporate healthcare chains, telemedicine platforms (Practo, 1mg, Tata Health), government hospitals (PHC-level), diagnostic centres.
work_environment.work_environment: Clinical and administrative environment inside hospital wards, OPDs, and clinics. Physical work involving patient mobility assistance, specimen collection, and equipment sterilisation. Shift work common. Emotionally and physically demanding.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own home healthcare or clinic support services.

careers.title: Press Officer
careers.description: A Press Officer (also Media Relations Manager or Communications Officer) manages an organisation's relationship with the news media — writing press releases, organising press conferences, responding to journalist queries, crafting media statements, and monitoring news coverage. In India, Press Officers work for government bodies (Ministry media cells, PIB — Press Information Bureau), political parties, corporations (Tata, Reliance, Infosys), NGOs, educational institutions, entertainment companies, and sports organisations (BCCI). The role requires deep knowledge of Indian print, TV, digital, and radio media landscapes. Senior Press Officers manage crisis communications when organisations face negative publicity.
personality_traits: Excellent written and verbal communication
Media landscape knowledge (print, TV, digital, radio)
Relationship-building with journalists and editors
Composure under media pressure
Strategic messaging and narrative control
Crisis communication ability
Research and fact-checking rigour
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (Journalism/Mass Communication or Arts/Humanities strongly preferred); MBA Communications or IIMC PG Diploma is the professional standard
entrance_exams: IIMC entrance exam (for PG Diploma in Journalism/Advertising/PR)
CUET (for BA Journalism/Mass Communication)
Symbiosis Entrance Test (SET) for Symbiosis SIMC Pune
MICAT (MICA Ahmedabad) — for advertising and strategic communication
AJK-MCRC Jamia Millia Islamia entrance
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream (Humanities preferred).
2. BA in Journalism/Mass Communication/Public Relations.
3. PG Diploma in Advertising and Public Relations (IIMC) OR MBA in Communications (MICA, SIMC, Symbiosis).
4. Internship at PR agency (Genesis BCW, MSL, Edelman India, Adfactors PR) or corporate communications team.
5. Entry: PR Executive → Communications Executive → Press Officer → Head of Communications → Director of Public Affairs.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: BA Journalism/Mass Communication (Govt): INR 20,000–1,00,000/yr; IIMC PG Diploma: INR 55,000–1,00,000/yr; MBA Communications (MICA): INR 10,00,000–15,00,000 total; MBA Communications Private: INR 2,00,000–8,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: PR Executive → Communications Executive → Press Officer / Media Relations Manager → Senior Press Officer → Head of Media Relations → Director of Corporate Communications → VP Communications / CPCO (Chief Public Communications Officer)

work_environment.places_of_work: Government ministry media cells (PIB — Press Information Bureau), corporate communications departments, PR agencies (Genesis BCW, MSL, Edelman India, Adfactors PR), NGOs, educational institutions, sports organisations (BCCI).
work_environment.work_environment: Office and media event environment; mix of desk writing work and journalist interactions. On-call during crises or major announcements. High-pressure deadlines aligned to news cycles. Requires always-on media monitoring.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own PR and media relations consultancy.

careers.title: AI Content Trainer / Prompt Engineer
careers.description: An AI Content Trainer (also called Prompt Engineer, AI Trainer, or RLHF Data Annotator) works with artificial intelligence companies to improve the quality, accuracy, safety, and cultural alignment of large language models (LLMs) like GPT, Gemini, Claude, and Llama. The work involves writing high-quality model prompts, evaluating AI-generated responses, creating training data for reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), detecting biases and errors, and writing diverse content samples for model fine-tuning. In India, AI Content Trainers are employed or contracted by global AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Scale AI, Appen, Remotasks, DataAnnotation.tech) through remote contracts. This is a rapidly emerging career created by the generative AI boom of 2022–2025. Indian English and multilingual proficiency make India a key hub for AI training work.
personality_traits: Excellent English writing and language analysis skills
Critical evaluation of AI responses (accuracy, bias, safety)
Logical and structured thinking for prompt design
Domain expertise in target field (coding, law, medicine, etc.)
Multilingual ability (valued for Indian language LLM training)
Patience for iterative feedback work
Ethical sensitivity about AI-generated content
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; strong English writing ability is the primary requirement; Science/Engineering background helps for technical AI training roles
entrance_exams: No formal national exam; AI company-specific recruitment tests (language proficiency, analytical writing, domain knowledge assessment)
No mandatory certification; DeepLearning.AI Prompt Engineering courses (Coursera) valued
Andrew Ng's AI courses (Coursera) relevant for technical AI trainer roles
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream (strong English proficiency essential).
2. Graduate in any discipline (English/Journalism/Engineering/Law/Medicine — domain expertise valued).
3. Complete free online AI/ML fundamentals courses (DeepLearning.AI, Google AI Essentials — free on Coursera).
4. Learn Prompt Engineering: OpenAI Prompt Engineering Guide (free), DeepLearning.AI Short Course on Prompt Engineering.
5. Apply to AI training platforms: Scale AI, Appen, Remotasks, DataAnnotation.tech.
6. Build expertise in a specific domain (coding, medical, legal, multilingual) for higher-value training work.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: No mandatory course fees; DeepLearning.AI Prompt Engineering Short Courses: free; Google AI Essentials (Coursera): free–INR 2,000; Domain-specific courses: INR 5,000–50,000; Computer and internet connection: primary investment
careers.career_growth_path: AI Data Annotator → AI Content Trainer → Senior AI Trainer → Lead Prompt Engineer → Prompt Engineering Manager → AI Alignment Researcher → Head of AI Safety and Quality

work_environment.places_of_work: Remote work for global AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind via contractors), AI outsourcing platforms (Scale AI, Appen, Remotasks, DataAnnotation.tech), Indian AI startups (Sarvam AI, Krutrim, Bhashini) needing Indian language training data.
work_environment.work_environment: Fully remote, home-based, or co-working space; flexible hours. Screen-intensive reading, writing, and evaluation work. Freelance or contract-based structure common. Growing from gig work to full-time roles at AI companies.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Inherently flexible — can build own AI training and dataset creation services company.

careers.title: Learning and Development Manager
careers.description: A Learning and Development (L&D) Manager designs, delivers, and manages training programmes and talent development initiatives to improve the skills, performance, and career growth of employees within an organisation. In India, L&D Managers are employed across IT companies, banking (RBI mandates continuous financial literacy training), manufacturing, hospitality (NSDC training obligations), FMCG, and consulting firms. They design onboarding programmes, identify skills gaps, manage learning management systems (LMS — Cornerstone, Docebo, TalentLMS), arrange e-learning modules, and evaluate training ROI. India's large workforce upskilling challenge — with the National Skills Development policy requiring 400 million workers to be skilled by 2022 — has created significant L&D leadership demand.
personality_traits: Instructional design and adult learning expertise
Communication and facilitation skills
Programme management and coordination
Technology proficiency with LMS platforms
Business acumen to align L&D with company goals
Data analysis to measure training ROI
Empathy and understanding of learner needs
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream (Commerce/Social Sciences/Education preferred); MBA HR or M.Ed is the standard professional pathway
entrance_exams: CAT / XAT (for MBA HR / Learning and Development specialisation)
TISS NET (for MA Education or HR at TISS)
CUET (for BBA HR / B.Ed entry)
UGC-NET (Education — for academic L&D pathway)
No dedicated national L&D exam; CPTD (Certified Professional in Talent Development by ATD) is internationally recognised
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. Graduate in any discipline; B.Ed or BA Education helpful.
3. MBA in HR (XLRI, TISS, IIM) — most L&D Manager roles require MBA HR or equivalent.
4. Develop facilitation skills: public speaking, workshop design, case study facilitation.
5. Learn LMS platforms: Cornerstone, SAP SuccessFactors Learning, TalentLMS.
6. Certifications: ATD CPTD (Certified Professional in Talent Development), SHRM-CP, or ISTD (Indian Society for Training and Development) certifications.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: BBA/BA (Govt): INR 20,000–80,000/yr; MBA HR (XLRI/TISS): INR 5,00,000–25,00,000; MBA HR Private: INR 2,00,000–8,00,000; ATD CPTD exam: approx INR 40,000–80,000 (USD 500+); ISTD certification: INR 10,000–30,000
careers.career_growth_path: L&D Coordinator → L&D Executive → L&D Manager → Senior L&D Manager → Head of Learning and Development → Chief Learning Officer (CLO) / VP Learning

work_environment.places_of_work: IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro — which run the world's largest corporate learning programmes), FMCG companies, banks (HDFC, ICICI), manufacturing companies, hospitality (Taj, Marriott India), consulting firms, NSDC-affiliated training centres.
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote/hybrid; mix of programme design (desk work), facilitation (classroom/virtual), and stakeholder meetings. Travel to company offices for training delivery. Collaborative with HR, business leaders, and external training vendors.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own corporate training and development consultancy or EdTech company.

careers.title: DevOps Engineer
careers.description: A DevOps Engineer bridges software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) by automating and streamlining the software delivery pipeline — from code commit to production deployment. They design and manage CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines, infrastructure as code (IaC), container orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker), and monitoring systems. In India, DevOps Engineers are among the highest-demand roles in the tech industry, employed at IT services companies, product startups, banks, and cloud-native companies. The Indian DevOps market is growing at 20%+ annually as enterprises accelerate digital transformation (NASSCOM 2024).
personality_traits: Systems and infrastructure thinking
Programming and scripting proficiency (Python, Bash, Go)
Automation-first mindset
Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Collaboration between development and operations teams
Problem-solving under production pressure
Continuous learning about new DevOps tools
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech CS/IT is the standard pathway
entrance_exams: JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech CS/IT)
GATE (CS/IT for M.Tech)
State engineering entrance exams
AWS/Azure/GCP Professional certifications — highly valued industry credentials
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Mathematics compulsory).
2. B.Tech CS/IT or BCA/B.Sc CS.
3. Learn Linux, networking, scripting (Python, Bash).
4. Master Docker and Kubernetes (container orchestration).
5. Learn CI/CD tools: Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI.
6. Cloud certifications: AWS DevOps Professional, Azure DevOps Engineer Expert, Google Professional DevOps Engineer.
7. Learn IaC: Terraform, Ansible, CloudFormation.
8. Entry: Junior DevOps Engineer at IT company or startup.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt NIT/IIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; Cloud DevOps certifications (AWS/Azure): INR 10,000–25,000 each; Kubernetes (CKAD/CKA) certification: INR 20,000–30,000; Bootcamps/online courses: INR 5,000–1,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior DevOps Engineer → DevOps Engineer → Senior DevOps Engineer → Lead DevOps Engineer → DevOps Architect → Head of Platform Engineering → VP of Engineering

work_environment.places_of_work: IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL), product startups, fintech companies, cloud-native organisations, SaaS companies across Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Pune, Mumbai, Gurugram.
work_environment.work_environment: Remote or office; highly technical. On-call rotation for production incidents. Collaborative with development, QA, and security teams. Fast-paced with continuous deployment cycles.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own DevOps consulting or platform engineering services company.

careers.title: Mobile App Developer
careers.description: A Mobile App Developer designs and builds applications for smartphones and tablets using iOS (Swift, Objective-C), Android (Kotlin, Java), or cross-platform frameworks (React Native, Flutter, Xamarin). In India, with 750+ million smartphone users and one of the world's highest app download rates (25+ billion downloads annually on Google Play), Mobile App Developers are in constant high demand. They work at IT companies, product startups (Flipkart, Ola, CRED, Groww), banks building mobile banking apps, healthcare companies (Practo, Tata 1mg), and EdTech firms (BYJU'S, Unacademy). Flutter (developed by Google) is particularly popular in India's startup ecosystem.
personality_traits: Mobile UI/UX design sensibility
Programming proficiency (Kotlin/Swift/Dart)
Performance optimisation for mobile hardware
Cross-platform development thinking
API integration skills
Testing on diverse device ecosystems
Continuous learning for rapidly evolving mobile platforms
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech CS/IT or BCA is standard; any stream for bootcamp pathway
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CS/IT)
State engineering entrance exams
No mandatory national exam for bootcamp pathway; company aptitude test
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. B.Tech CS/IT or BCA/B.Sc CS (formal route) OR mobile development bootcamp.
3. Choose platform: Android (Kotlin) or iOS (Swift) or Cross-platform (Flutter/React Native).
4. Build 5–8 published apps on Google Play Store or Apple App Store.
5. Learn REST APIs, Firebase, SQLite, push notifications, and app analytics.
6. Certifications: Google Associate Android Developer certification; Apple Developer certification.
7. Entry: Junior Mobile Developer at IT company or startup.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; BCA (Govt): INR 20,000–80,000/yr; Mobile development bootcamp: INR 30,000–1,50,000; Google Android certification: INR 5,000–15,000; Online courses (Udemy): INR 500–5,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Mobile Developer → Mobile App Developer → Senior Mobile Developer → Lead Mobile Engineer → Mobile Architect → Head of Mobile Engineering → VP of Product Engineering

work_environment.places_of_work: IT companies, product startups (Ola, Swiggy, CRED, Groww, PhonePe), banks building mobile banking apps, healthcare companies (Practo, 1mg), EdTech firms, gaming companies.
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; desk-based development. Collaborative with designers, backend developers, and product managers. App release cycles with Apple App Store and Google Play review timelines.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can build and monetise own app or start a mobile app development studio.

careers.title: Ethical Hacker / Penetration Tester
careers.description: An Ethical Hacker (also Penetration Tester or Security Researcher) is a cybersecurity professional who is legally authorised to attempt to break into computer systems, networks, and applications to identify vulnerabilities before malicious hackers can exploit them. They use the same tools and techniques as cybercriminals — but with explicit permission and a goal of improving security. In India, with 1.4 million cybersecurity incidents reported to CERT-In in 2023 and a shortage of 790,000 cybersecurity professionals (NASSCOM), Ethical Hackers are among the most sought-after IT security professionals. They work for banks, IT companies, government agencies (DRDO, NIC, CERT-In), and cybersecurity consulting firms.
personality_traits: Analytical and investigative mindset
Deep knowledge of networking and operating systems
Persistence and creative problem-solving
Ethical integrity (working within legal bounds)
Knowledge of attack and defence techniques
Programming and scripting ability (Python, C)
Continuous learning about new vulnerabilities
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech CS/IT or ECE is the standard pathway
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CS/IT/ECE)
GATE (CS for M.Tech Cybersecurity)
EC-Council CEH (Certified Ethical Hacker) exam — most recognised in India
OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional) — most respected globally
CompTIA Security+ — entry-level security certification
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Mathematics compulsory).
2. B.Tech CS/IT/ECE or BCA/B.Sc CS.
3. Master networking: TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS, firewalls, VPNs.
4. Learn Linux (Kali Linux — primary ethical hacking OS), Python, Bash.
5. Practice: TryHackMe, HackTheBox, VulnHub platforms.
6. CEH (EC-Council Certified Ethical Hacker) certification — widely valued in India for IT sector jobs.
7. OSCP (Offensive Security Certified Professional) — most respected globally for pen testing.
8. Participate in Bug Bounty programmes (HackerOne, Bugcrowd) — Google, Facebook, Microsoft pay for valid vulnerabilities.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt NIT/IIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; CEH certification (EC-Council): INR 30,000–50,000; OSCP certification (OffSec): approx INR 60,000–80,000 (USD 999); CompTIA Security+: INR 15,000–25,000; Online prep (TryHackMe premium): INR 1,500/month
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Security Analyst → Penetration Tester → Senior Pen Tester → Lead Security Researcher → Security Architect → CISO (Chief Information Security Officer) / Bug Bounty Hunter (independent)

work_environment.places_of_work: Cybersecurity consulting firms (Deloitte India, EY India, KPMG India, PwC India), IT companies (Infosys Cyber Security, TCS Cyber, Wipro Cybersecurity), banks, government agencies (CERT-In, DRDO, NIC), fintech companies.
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; technical lab-like environment. Project-based engagement work. May work on active client systems during authorised testing windows. High concentration and methodical work.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can build career as an independent security consultant or bug bounty hunter.

careers.title: Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
careers.description: A Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) applies software engineering principles to IT operations problems — writing code to automate tasks, building systems that scale reliably, measuring and improving service performance (SLAs/SLOs/SLIs), and responding to incidents. Coined by Google in the early 2000s, SRE has become a global standard for operating large-scale internet services. In India, SREs are employed at product companies (Flipkart, Razorpay, Zomato, PhonePe, MakeMyTrip), tech giants' India offices (Google, Microsoft, Amazon), and large IT companies adopting SRE practices. SREs at top Indian product companies command salaries comparable to senior software engineers.
personality_traits: Systems thinking at scale
Programming proficiency (Python, Go, Java)
Statistical thinking for reliability metrics
Calm under production incident pressure
Infrastructure and networking knowledge
Automation-first mindset
Documentation discipline (runbooks, post-mortems)
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech CS/IT is the standard pathway
entrance_exams: JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech CS/IT)
GATE (CS for M.Tech/PSU route)
Google SRE certification — Google Cloud Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer
Linux Foundation CKA (Certified Kubernetes Administrator)
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Mathematics compulsory).
2. B.Tech CS/IT or B.Sc CS.
3. Build strong software engineering foundation (Python, Go, or Java).
4. Master Linux systems administration, networking, and distributed systems.
5. Study Google's free SRE Book (sre.google — free online).
6. Learn observability tools: Prometheus, Grafana, Datadog, New Relic.
7. Master Kubernetes (CKA certification) and cloud platforms.
8. Entry: Junior SRE or DevOps Engineer transitioning to SRE role.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt IIT/NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; CKA (Kubernetes) certification: INR 20,000–30,000; Google Professional DevOps Engineer certification: INR 15,000–25,000; Postgraduate cloud programmes: INR 80,000–2,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior SRE → SRE → Senior SRE → Staff SRE → Principal SRE → Director of Reliability Engineering → VP of Infrastructure

work_environment.places_of_work: Product companies (Flipkart, Razorpay, Zomato, PhonePe, CRED, Meesho, MakeMyTrip), global tech companies' India offices (Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Uber, Airbnb), fintech and healthcare tech.
work_environment.work_environment: Remote or office; technical and on-call intensive. Incident response can require off-hours attention. Deeply collaborative with development and infrastructure teams. High responsibility and high compensation.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can consult for organisations building reliability engineering practices.

careers.title: RPA Developer
careers.description: A Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Developer designs and builds software robots (bots) that automate repetitive, rule-based business tasks — such as data entry, invoice processing, report generation, and system integrations — using platforms like UiPath, Automation Anywhere, and Blue Prism. In India, RPA is one of the fastest-growing automation technologies with the Indian RPA market growing at 35% CAGR (NASSCOM 2024). RPA Developers are employed in IT services companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro have dedicated RPA practices), banking and financial services (where automation of back-office processes generates massive cost savings), insurance, healthcare, and manufacturing.
personality_traits: Logical and algorithmic thinking
Attention to process documentation and detail
Programming aptitude (Python, VBScript, .NET)
Business process analysis skills
Problem-solving for exception handling in automation
Collaboration with business analysts and clients
Continuous learning about new automation platforms
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) or Commerce; B.Tech CS/IT is most common; any stream for non-programming RPA roles
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CS/IT — engineering pathway)
State engineering entrance exams
No dedicated national RPA exam; UiPath Certified Professional Developer (UIARD) is the gold standard certification
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream (Science/Mathematics preferred).
2. B.Tech CS/IT or BCA or B.Sc CS.
3. Learn RPA fundamentals: UiPath (most popular in India), Automation Anywhere, Blue Prism.
4. UiPath Academy: free online courses and certification — academy.uipath.com.
5. Study business process documentation, workflow diagrams, and exception handling.
6. UiPath RPA Associate (UIARA) → UiPath RPA Developer (UIARD) → Advanced Developer.
7. Entry: Junior RPA Developer at IT companies, BPO/KPO firms, or banking clients.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; UiPath Academy (free online): free; UiPath UIARD certification exam: INR 10,000–20,000 (USD 150); Automation Anywhere University (online): free; Commercial RPA bootcamps: INR 15,000–60,000
careers.career_growth_path: RPA Intern → Junior RPA Developer → RPA Developer → Senior RPA Developer → RPA Lead → RPA Architect → Head of Automation / Director of Intelligent Automation

work_environment.places_of_work: IT services companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Accenture India — all have large RPA practices), banking and insurance companies, manufacturing, healthcare, and shared services centres (HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, SBI automation teams).
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; process analysis and bot development work. Collaborative with business users and IT teams. Mix of technical scripting and process consulting.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can offer own RPA implementation consulting for SMEs.

careers.title: Computer Vision Engineer
careers.description: A Computer Vision Engineer develops systems and algorithms that enable computers to interpret and understand visual information from the world — images and video — to perform tasks like object detection, facial recognition, medical image analysis, autonomous vehicle navigation, and quality inspection in manufacturing. In India, Computer Vision is a critical skill for the country's AI ecosystem — with applications in agriculture (crop disease detection), healthcare (diabetic retinopathy screening), surveillance (smart city cameras), manufacturing quality control, and autonomous systems. India's ISRO, DRDO, and a growing ecosystem of AI startups (Uncanny Vision, Mad Street Den, Staqu) are key employers.
personality_traits: Strong mathematical foundation (linear algebra, calculus, statistics)
Deep learning and neural network proficiency
Python programming and OpenCV expertise
Creative problem-solving for visual recognition challenges
Attention to model accuracy and dataset quality
Research orientation
GPU computing aptitude
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics — Mathematics and Physics compulsory)
entrance_exams: JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech CS/IT/ECE)
GATE (CS/ECE — for M.Tech Computer Vision / AI)
IIT JAM (Mathematics/Physics — for MSc entry pathway)
CSIR-NET (Mathematical Sciences/Physical Sciences — for research fellowship)
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (PCM essential).
2. B.Tech CS/ECE (preferred) or B.Sc Mathematics/Physics.
3. Master Python, NumPy, OpenCV, TensorFlow/PyTorch.
4. Specialise: deep learning for images (CNNs — Convolutional Neural Networks), object detection (YOLO, R-CNN), image segmentation.
5. Build projects: ADAS systems, medical imaging classifiers, security cameras.
6. M.Tech/M.S. in Computer Vision or AI at IIT/IIIT/IISc strongly valued for research roles.
7. Kaggle competitions: vision track (Image Classification, Object Detection challenges).
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/ECE (Govt IIT/NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; M.Tech AI/CV (Govt): INR 30,000–1,50,000/yr; Online DL courses (DeepLearning.AI, fast.ai): free–INR 50,000; NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute courses: INR 5,000–30,000
careers.career_growth_path: Computer Vision Intern → Junior CV Engineer → Computer Vision Engineer → Senior CV Engineer → Lead Computer Vision Scientist → Principal Research Scientist → Head of AI Vision Engineering

work_environment.places_of_work: AI startups (Uncanny Vision, Mad Street Den, Staqu, SigTuple), ISRO, DRDO, global tech companies' India labs (Google India Research, Microsoft Research India, Samsung Research), automotive companies, healthcare AI firms.
work_environment.work_environment: Research lab or office environment; GPU computing infrastructure essential. Mix of algorithm design, model training, and production deployment. Collaborative with data scientists, software engineers, and domain experts.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own computer vision AI company.

careers.title: Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engineer
careers.description: A Natural Language Processing (NLP) Engineer builds AI systems that understand, process, and generate human language — powering chatbots, virtual assistants, machine translation, sentiment analysis, document summarisation, and information extraction. In India, NLP is critically important given the country's 22 official scheduled languages and hundreds of dialects — creating unique demand for multilingual NLP models. Initiatives like AI4Bharat (IIT Madras), ULCA (Government of India), and Bhashini are building India-specific NLP capabilities. NLP Engineers work at tech companies (Google India Research, Microsoft Research India), AI startups, banks (fraud detection via NLP), and legal/healthcare companies.
personality_traits: Strong Python and deep learning proficiency
Linguistic curiosity and language understanding
Experience with transformer models (BERT, GPT, T5)
Text preprocessing and feature engineering skills
Research orientation for novel NLP challenges
Knowledge of Indian languages (strongly valued in India)
Collaboration with linguists and domain experts
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics — Mathematics compulsory); Linguistics background is increasingly valued
entrance_exams: JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech CS/IT)
GATE (CS for M.Tech AI/NLP)
IIT JAM (Mathematics for MSc)
CSIR-NET (Mathematical/Computer Sciences for research fellowship)
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Mathematics essential).
2. B.Tech CS/IT or B.Sc Mathematics/Statistics.
3. Build strong Python and deep learning foundation.
4. Study NLP specialisation: Hugging Face transformers, NLTK, spaCy, Gensim.
5. Learn about Indian language NLP: AI4Bharat models (IndicBERT, Indic Whisper), BharatGPT.
6. Work on NLP projects: sentiment analysis, chatbots, named entity recognition, machine translation.
7. Contribute to AI4Bharat or ULCA datasets — builds both skills and reputation.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt IIT/NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; M.Tech AI/NLP (Govt): INR 30,000–1,50,000/yr; Hugging Face course (free): free; DeepLearning.AI NLP Specialisation (Coursera): free audit; Bootcamps: INR 30,000–1,50,000
careers.career_growth_path: NLP Intern / Research Intern → Junior NLP Engineer → NLP Engineer → Senior NLP Engineer → Lead NLP Research Scientist → Principal Scientist (NLP) → Head of NLP Research

work_environment.places_of_work: AI research labs (Google India Research, Microsoft Research India, Amazon Alexa team), AI startups (Sarvam AI, Krutrim, Yellow.ai, Vernacular.ai, AI4Bharat consortium), banks, healthcare AI companies, legal tech firms.
work_environment.work_environment: Research lab or office; intensive model training and evaluation work. Collaborative with linguists, data annotators, and product teams. Access to high-performance computing essential.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own NLP product company, particularly for Indian language AI.

careers.title: Data Architect
careers.description: A Data Architect designs and manages an organisation's data infrastructure — defining how data is collected, stored, transformed, integrated, and accessed across enterprise systems. They create data models, establish data governance standards, design data warehouses and data lakes, and ensure data quality and security at scale. In India, with the explosion of big data across banking (RBI's data requirements), telecom (Jio, Airtel handling petabytes of data), e-commerce (Amazon India, Flipkart), and government (Aadhaar, DigiLocker, UPI ecosystem), Data Architects are among the most valued senior data professionals. They bridge IT architecture with business strategy.
personality_traits: Strong data modelling and architecture expertise
Enterprise-level systems thinking
Knowledge of cloud data platforms (AWS Redshift, Azure Synapse, Snowflake)
SQL and NoSQL database mastery
Data governance and quality management
Communication with both technical and business stakeholders
Strategic thinking for long-term data infrastructure
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Mathematics/Statistics) or Commerce; B.Tech CS/IT is the standard pathway; M.Tech or MBA helps for senior architect roles
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CS/IT)
GATE (CS/IT)
AWS Certified Data Analytics Specialty or Google Professional Data Engineer — highly valued
No dedicated national Data Architect exam; enterprise certifications (AWS, Azure, GCP) are key
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science/Commerce (Mathematics essential).
2. B.Tech CS/IT or B.Sc CS/Statistics.
3. Build 8–10 years of data engineering, database administration, or analytics experience.
4. Learn data modelling: dimensional modelling (Kimball), data vault, entity-relationship.
5. Master cloud data platforms: Snowflake, AWS Redshift, Azure Synapse, Google BigQuery, Databricks.
6. Study data governance frameworks: DAMA-DMBOK, GDPR compliance, PDPB (India's data protection law).
7. AWS Certified Data Analytics Specialty or Google Professional Data Engineer certification.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt IIT/NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; AWS Data Analytics Specialty: INR 15,000–25,000; Google Professional Data Engineer: INR 15,000–25,000; Databricks Certified Data Engineer: INR 15,000–25,000; Senior-level online training: INR 20,000–1,50,000
careers.career_growth_path: Data Analyst → Data Engineer → Senior Data Engineer → Data Architect → Principal Data Architect → Chief Data Architect → Chief Data Officer (CDO)

work_environment.places_of_work: Large IT companies (TCS, Infosys, Wipro enterprise data practices), banking and financial services (HDFC, ICICI, SBI data warehousing teams), telecom (Jio, Airtel), e-commerce (Amazon India, Flipkart), government data platforms (NIC, UIDAI).
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; senior role with significant design, documentation, and stakeholder management. Participates in enterprise architecture boards. Translates business requirements into technical data blueprints.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can offer own data architecture consulting for enterprises.

careers.title: GIS Analyst
careers.description: A Geographic Information Systems (GIS) Analyst collects, manages, analyses, and visualises spatial and geographic data to support decision-making in urban planning, disaster management, agriculture, defence, infrastructure, environment, and public health. In India, GIS Analysts are employed by the Survey of India, ISRO's National Remote Sensing Centre (NRSC), state urban planning departments, NHAI (road infrastructure), NDMA (disaster management), forest departments, and private companies providing location intelligence. India's Smart City programme, SVAMITVA drone mapping scheme, and PM Gati Shakti masterplan are all driven by GIS data, creating significant employment.
personality_traits: Spatial thinking and geographic curiosity
Proficiency with GIS software (ArcGIS, QGIS)
Data analysis and visualisation skills
Remote sensing interpretation ability
Attention to accuracy in spatial data
Knowledge of cartographic principles
Field data collection adaptability
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Geography/Mathematics preferred); any stream accepted for vocational/certificate entry
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech Geo-informatics/Civil — formal engineering pathway)
GATE (Geo-informatics and Survey Engineering — GE)
CUET (for B.Sc Geography / B.Sc Geoinformatics)
Survey of India / ISRO NRSC direct recruitment exams
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Geography, Mathematics preferred).
2. B.Sc Geoinformatics / B.Sc Geography / B.Tech Geo-informatics.
3. Master GIS software: ArcGIS (Esri — industry standard), QGIS (free, open-source), Google Earth Engine.
4. Learn remote sensing: satellite imagery analysis, LIDAR, drone surveys.
5. Python for GIS: geopandas, Shapely, Folium libraries.
6. Certifications: Esri Technical Certification, ArcGIS Pro Certification.
7. Entry: GIS Analyst at government mapping agencies, infrastructure companies, or urban planning firms.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Sc Geoinformatics (Govt): INR 20,000–1,00,000/yr; B.Tech Geo-informatics (Govt NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; Esri India ArcGIS Training: INR 15,000–60,000; QGIS (free software): no cost; Short GIS courses (IIRS Dehradun): INR 5,000–30,000
careers.career_growth_path: GIS Technician → GIS Analyst → Senior GIS Analyst → GIS Lead → GIS Manager → Head of Geospatial Analytics → Chief Geospatial Officer

work_environment.places_of_work: Survey of India (Dehradun), ISRO NRSC (Hyderabad), state urban planning departments, NHAI, NDMA, forest departments, urban local bodies, GIS consulting firms, defence mapping (Army Map Service), infrastructure companies.
work_environment.work_environment: Mix of office GIS data analysis and field data collection. Computer-intensive mapping work. May involve travel to survey sites. Government roles offer stability; private sector offers technology exposure.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own GIS consulting and spatial analytics firm.

careers.title: IT Auditor
careers.description: An IT Auditor evaluates an organisation's information technology infrastructure, policies, procedures, and controls to ensure they comply with regulatory requirements, protect data, and operate efficiently. IT Auditors work in two main contexts: internal IT auditors who work within organisations (banks, IT companies, PSUs) and external IT auditors employed by audit and consulting firms. In India, SEBI, RBI, and IRDA regulations mandate IT audits for financial institutions. IT Auditors increasingly work with frameworks like COBIT, ISO 27001, SOC 2, and India's IT Act 2000 / PDPB (Personal Data Protection Bill). ISACA's CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor) is the most valued certification.
personality_traits: Analytical and evaluative thinking
Attention to compliance and governance detail
Knowledge of IT systems and security controls
Communication with auditees and management
Report writing and documentation skills
Integrity and independence
Continuous learning about new technologies and regulations
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) or Commerce; B.Tech CS/IT is most common; CA/CMA background adds value for financial IT audit
entrance_exams: JEE Main (for B.Tech CS/IT — technical pathway)
CA Foundation/Intermediate (ICAI — for financial IT audit roles)
CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor by ISACA — globally recognised gold standard)
No dedicated national IT Auditor exam; CISA is the primary professional credential
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science/Commerce.
2. B.Tech CS/IT or B.Com/CA (ICAI) for financial IT audit.
3. Build 2–3 years of IT or internal audit experience.
4. Obtain CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor by ISACA) — most valued in India for IT audit roles.
5. Study COBIT 2019 framework, ISO 27001, RBI IT framework for banks.
6. Learn audit tools: ACL Analytics, TeamMate, IDEA (audit data analytics tools).
7. Entry: IT Audit Associate → IT Auditor → Senior IT Auditor → IT Audit Manager.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; CA Foundation to Final (ICAI): approx INR 60,000–80,000 total; CISA certification (ISACA): approx INR 30,000–50,000 (USD 575 for ISACA members); COBIT 2019 training: INR 15,000–40,000; Short IT audit courses: INR 10,000–50,000
careers.career_growth_path: IT Audit Associate → IT Auditor → Senior IT Auditor → IT Audit Manager → Director of IT Audit → Chief Audit Executive / CISO (parallel path)

work_environment.places_of_work: Big 4 and consulting firms (Deloitte, EY, KPMG, PwC — all have large IT audit practices in India), banks (RBI mandate), insurance companies (IRDA mandate), IT companies (Infosys, TCS internal audit), government PSUs.
work_environment.work_environment: Office-based with travel to client sites for audit fieldwork. Mix of technical system analysis and governance review. Deadline-driven during audit cycles. Intellectually rigorous and compliance-focused.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can set up own IT audit and cybersecurity compliance consultancy.

careers.title: Telecommunications Engineer
careers.description: A Telecommunications Engineer designs, installs, and manages communication systems — including mobile networks (4G/5G), broadband infrastructure, satellite communications, optical fibre networks, and IPTV systems. In India, the telecom sector is one of the world's largest — with Jio, Airtel, and BSNL operating networks serving 1.1+ billion subscribers. India's National Broadband Mission (BharatNet), 5G rollout (India's 5G was launched in 2022 and now covers 700+ cities), and the Satellite Internet revolution (Starlink, OneWeb) are creating thousands of engineering jobs. Telecom Engineers also work in ISRO's satellite communication division and the Indian Navy's communication systems.
personality_traits: Technical aptitude for RF, signal processing, and networking
Mathematical modelling of communication systems
Problem-solving for network coverage and capacity
Knowledge of telecom standards (3GPP, IEEE 802.11)
Field adaptability for tower and infrastructure installation
Safety consciousness for high-altitude tower work
Continuous learning about evolving network technologies
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics — all essential)
entrance_exams: JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech ECE/Electronics and Communication Engineering)
GATE (ECE — Electronics and Communication Engineering) for M.Tech/PSU
State engineering entrance exams (MHT-CET, KCET, AP-EAMCET, WBJEE)
BSNL / BSNL JE Exam (for government telecom engineer recruitment)
UPSC ESE (Engineering Services Examination — for DoT/BSNL Group A officers)
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (PCM essential).
2. B.E./B.Tech in Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE) — primary pathway.
3. Study telecom-specific subjects: antenna theory, microwave engineering, optical fibre, digital communications, mobile networks.
4. Learn telecom standards: 3GPP (for 4G/5G), IEEE 802.11 (WiFi), ETSI.
5. Gain exposure: internship at Jio, Airtel, Ericsson India, Nokia India, Cisco India.
6. GATE ECE for M.Tech (VLSI/Communication Engineering) or PSU entry (BSNL, TCIL, Bharat Electronics).
7. Cisco CCNA (for IP networking aspects of telecom) is a useful complementary certification.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech ECE (Govt IIT/NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; M.Tech ECE/Communication Engineering (Govt): INR 30,000–1,50,000/yr; Cisco CCNA: INR 20,000–30,000; GATE ECE preparation courses: INR 20,000–1,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Telecom Engineer → Telecom Engineer → Senior Telecom Engineer → Network Planning Engineer → Principal Engineer → Telecom Manager → Director of Network Engineering → Chief Technology Officer (Telecom)

work_environment.places_of_work: Telecom operators (Jio, Airtel, BSNL, Vodafone Idea), telecom equipment vendors (Ericsson India, Nokia India, Huawei India, Cisco India, Samsung Networks India), ISRO (satellite communications), Indian Navy (defence communications), TRAI (regulatory body).
work_environment.work_environment: Mix of office design work and field-based tower and infrastructure installation. Travel to remote sites for BharatNet rural connectivity projects. Indoor office work for network planning and optimisation.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own telecom infrastructure services or tower management company.

careers.title: Technical Program Manager
careers.description: A Technical Program Manager (TPM) drives the execution of large, complex technical projects and programmes — coordinating multiple engineering teams, managing dependencies, setting timelines, navigating technical trade-offs, and ensuring deliverables meet strategic objectives. Unlike a Product Manager (who focuses on what to build), a TPM focuses on how to build it at scale. In India, TPMs are valued at large technology companies (Microsoft India, Google India, Amazon India, Flipkart), where products require coordinating dozens of engineering teams across multiple time zones. The TPM role is a distinct career track that combines technical depth with programme management skills, and is typically a senior role for experienced engineers.
personality_traits: Technical depth in software engineering or infrastructure
Programme and project management expertise
Ambiguity resolution and decision-making
Cross-team communication and influence
Risk identification and mitigation
Data-driven approach to delivery tracking
Leadership of engineering teams without direct authority
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics); B.Tech CS/IT is the required foundation; MBA optional but valued
entrance_exams: JEE Main / Advanced (for B.Tech CS/IT)
PMP (Project Management Professional by PMI) — valued credential
No dedicated TPM national exam; companies assess via system design and engineering management interviews
CAT / GMAT (for MBA — optional senior pathway)
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (Mathematics compulsory).
2. B.Tech CS/IT or equivalent engineering degree.
3. Build 5–8 years of software engineering experience.
4. Develop programme management skills: project planning, dependency management, stakeholder communication.
5. PMP (PMI) certification — adds credibility for large programme management roles.
6. Entry: Senior Software Engineer / Tech Lead → Associate TPM → Technical Program Manager.
7. FAANG companies (Google, Amazon, Microsoft, Meta India) hire TPMs through specialised interviews.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Tech CS/IT (Govt IIT/NIT): INR 50,000–2,50,000/yr; PMP certification: INR 30,000–40,000; MBA (optional): INR 2,00,000–23,00,000; TPM interview preparation courses: INR 5,000–30,000
careers.career_growth_path: Senior Software Engineer → Tech Lead → Associate TPM → Technical Program Manager → Senior TPM → Principal TPM → Director of Technical Programme Management → VP of Engineering

work_environment.places_of_work: Large tech companies (Microsoft India, Google India, Amazon India, Meta India, Apple India), large product companies (Flipkart, Razorpay, Swiggy, PhonePe), IT services companies (Infosys, TCS — senior delivery managers).
work_environment.work_environment: Office or remote; mix of technical architecture reviews, programme planning, team meetings, and stakeholder presentations. High-influence role; requires navigating complex organisational dynamics at senior levels.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can transition to consulting or startup founding leveraging programme management expertise.
