careers.title: Arts Administrator
careers.description: An Arts Administrator manages the operational, financial, programming, and organisational aspects of an arts or cultural organisation — from budgets, fundraising, and grant writing to programming, artist relations, marketing, and audience development. India's cultural ecosystem includes government bodies (Sangeet Natak Akademi, Lalit Kala Akademi, Sahitya Akademi, Zonal Cultural Centres), private organisations (Prithvi Theatre, Ranga Shankara, India Habitat Centre, National Centre for Performing Arts Mumbai), museums, galleries, and literary festivals (Jaipur Literature Festival, Times Lit Fest, Kala Ghoda). Arts Administrators are the invisible backbone that makes cultural events, residencies, exhibitions, and performances happen.
personality_traits: Organisational management and financial administration
Grant writing for Ministry of Culture, Sangeet Natak Akademi, India Foundation for the Arts (IFA)
Programme development and curation
Artist and performer liaison
Marketing and audience development
Budgeting and resource management for cultural projects
Knowledge of India's cultural policy and funding landscape
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Humanities (History of Art, Cultural Studies, Mass Communication) background most relevant; no formal stream restriction
entrance_exams: No dedicated national arts administrator exam
TISS NET (for MA Cultural Studies pathway)
CUET (for BA Cultural Studies / BA Fine Arts / BA Mass Communication)
No mandatory certification; ISPA (International Society for Performing Arts) membership and arts management degrees are internationally valued
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. BA Cultural Studies / Fine Arts / Mass Communication or any humanities degree.
3. MA Arts Administration/Management (Nalanda University, SPJIMR Mumbai — limited options in India; international: Edinburgh, Goldsmiths, Columbia Arts Administration).
4. Internship at Prithvi Theatre, India Habitat Centre, NCPA, Ranga Shankara, or Jaipur Literature Festival.
5. Learn grant writing: India Foundation for the Arts (IFA) grant framework, Ministry of Culture application formats.
6. Build production coordination experience: volunteer at arts festivals, manage small productions.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: BA/MA Cultural Studies (Govt): INR 20,000-1,00,000/yr; SPJIMR Arts Management: INR 3,00,000-7,00,000; Internship at cultural organisation: typically unpaid initially; IFA grant writing workshop: INR 5,000-15,000; Arts Administrator starting salary: INR 15,000-50,000/month
careers.career_growth_path: Arts Programme Intern → Programme Coordinator → Arts Administrator → Senior Administrator → Programme Director → Artistic Director / Managing Director (Arts Organisation)

work_environment.places_of_work: Sangeet Natak Akademi, Lalit Kala Akademi, Sahitya Akademi (all government), NCPA (National Centre for Performing Arts Mumbai), India Habitat Centre Delhi, Prithvi Theatre Mumbai, Ranga Shankara Bengaluru, Jaipur Literature Festival, Serendipity Arts Foundation, India Foundation for the Arts, Aga Khan Trust for Culture India (arts programming), Godrej India Culture Lab.
work_environment.work_environment: Office, rehearsal space, gallery, or festival venue; mix of desk administration and on-site production management. High intensity during festival/exhibition periods; quieter in planning phases. Collaborative with artists, technicians, and funders.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can found own arts management organisation or cultural events company.

careers.title: Nutritionist / Clinical Dietitian
careers.description: A Nutritionist or Clinical Dietitian provides evidence-based dietary counselling and medical nutrition therapy for individuals across the lifespan — from managing diabetes, obesity, kidney disease, and eating disorders to supporting sports performance, paediatric nutrition, and public health nutrition programmes. In India, with the triple burden of malnutrition (undernutrition, micronutrient deficiency, and obesity — affecting 2/3 of the population in different ways), and the National Nutrition Mission (Poshan Abhiyaan), dietitians are critically needed. Clinical Dietitians work in hospitals (diabetes management, oncology nutrition, renal nutrition), community settings, corporate wellness, and food industry. The Dietetics Association of India (DAI) provides professional recognition. Note: this is distinct from Sports Nutritionist (ID 368) in clinical scope.
personality_traits: Clinical nutrition assessment (anthropometry, dietary recall, biochemistry interpretation)
Medical nutrition therapy for chronic diseases (diabetes, renal, hepatic, oncology)
Behaviour change communication for dietary counselling
Knowledge of FSSAI food labelling and nutritional standards
Community nutrition programme design
Menu planning for hospital therapeutic diets
Research skills for evidence-based practice
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Biology, Chemistry — both essential); B.Sc Dietetics/Nutrition/Home Science is the primary pathway; RD (Registered Dietitian) is the professional credential
entrance_exams: NEET UG (for medical college-affiliated BSc Dietetics — required at some institutions)
CUET (for B.Sc Dietetics / B.Sc Foods, Nutrition and Dietetics / B.Sc Home Science)
State allied health entrance exams
AIIH&PH Kolkata entrance for postgraduate public health nutrition
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (PCB; Biology and Chemistry essential).
2. B.Sc Dietetics or B.Sc Foods and Nutrition (4 years at medical college-affiliated programmes for clinical dietetics).
3. M.Sc Dietetics / M.Sc Food and Nutrition for specialisation and research.
4. Internship at hospital dietetics department — clinical rotation essential.
5. DAI (Dietetics Association of India) membership for professional recognition.
6. Specialise: Diabetic Educator, Renal Dietitian, Paediatric Dietitian, Sports Dietitian.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Sc Dietetics/Nutrition (Govt): INR 20,000-1,50,000/yr; M.Sc Dietetics (Govt): INR 20,000-1,50,000/yr; DAI membership: INR 2,000-5,000/yr; Diabetes Educator certification (RSSDI): INR 10,000-25,000; Renal Dietitian short courses: INR 5,000-20,000
careers.career_growth_path: Dietetics Intern → Junior Dietitian → Clinical Dietitian → Senior Dietitian → Chief Dietitian → Head of Nutrition Services → Director Nutrition and Wellness

work_environment.places_of_work: Hospital dietetics departments (AIIMS, Apollo, Fortis, Max, Manipal), public health nutrition (ICDS — Integrated Child Development Services under WCD, Poshan Abhiyaan field level), corporate wellness (Google India, TCS, Infosys cafeteria nutrition), food companies (R&D labelling compliance — Nestle India, HUL), community health programmes (NGOs, PHCs), private dietitian practice (diabetes, obesity, eating disorder clinics).
work_environment.work_environment: Hospital clinical environment or private clinic; mix of patient bedside consultations, cafeteria menu planning, and dietary assessment. Community nutrition roles involve field visits to AWCs (Anganwadi centres) and PHCs.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own private clinical nutrition practice or online nutrition consultancy.

careers.title: Interior Designer
careers.description: An Interior Designer creates functional, beautiful, and safe interior spaces for homes, offices, hotels, retail stores, restaurants, and healthcare facilities — developing design concepts, space plans, furniture specifications, material selections, lighting designs, and project budgets. India's real estate boom (INR 13+ lakh crore market, CREDAI 2024), the explosion of premium housing (luxury apartments in Mumbai, NCR, Bengaluru, Hyderabad), and commercial real estate expansion (co-working spaces, 5-star hotels, corporate offices) drive strong demand. Interior Designers work independently, at design studios (Studio Lotus, Abraham John Architects, Morphogenesis — design arms), hospitality companies (Taj Hotels design team), IKEA India, and large furniture brands. Note: Landscape Architect (ID 434) covers outdoor design separately.
personality_traits: Creative spatial visualisation and design thinking
Knowledge of materials, textures, colours, and lighting
AutoCAD, SketchUp, 3D Max, Lumion — design software proficiency
Client briefing, space planning, and project management
Knowledge of building codes (NBC — National Building Code) and accessibility standards
Budget management and vendor coordination for fit-out projects
Trend awareness in global and Indian design aesthetics
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Fine Arts or Design background (NID, NIFT) is most relevant; no formal stream restriction
entrance_exams: NATA (National Aptitude Test in Architecture — for Architecture-linked Interior Design programmes)
NID DAT (for NID Interior Space and Furniture Design)
UCEED (for IIT B.Des)
State university Fine Arts/Design entrance exams
No dedicated national Interior Designer exam; NID and CEPT offer the most respected Interior Design programmes
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. B.Des Interior Space and Furniture Design (NID Ahmedabad — India's most prestigious) or B.Arch (with interior design specialisation) or B.Des (Interior Design at CEPT, Symbiosis, Pearl Academy).
3. Learn design software: AutoCAD (2D space planning), SketchUp (3D modelling), 3ds Max (photorealistic rendering), Lumion (real-time VR rendering).
4. Build design portfolio: 10+ original interior design projects (residential, commercial, hospitality).
5. Internship at design studio or architecture firm.
6. IIID (Institute of Indian Interior Designers) membership — India's professional body for interior designers.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: NID B.Des Interior Space (Govt-subsidised): INR 50,000-2,50,000/yr; CEPT M.Des Interior Architecture: INR 1,00,000-3,00,000; Private interior design school: INR 1,00,000-5,00,000; AutoCAD/SketchUp training: INR 5,000-25,000; 3ds Max course: INR 10,000-40,000
careers.career_growth_path: Junior Interior Designer → Interior Designer → Senior Interior Designer → Principal Designer → Creative Director (Interior Design) → Own Design Studio Founder

work_environment.places_of_work: Interior design studios (Studio Lotus Delhi, Abraham & Thakore Design, Morphogenesis, Chromed Design Studio), hospitality design (Taj Hotels design team, Marriott India design, Oberoi Hotels), corporate office interior firms (Gensler India, HOK India, Perkins & Will India), real estate developers' design teams (DLF, Prestige, Godrej Properties), furniture brands (IKEA India design, Fabindia Home), independent practice.
work_environment.work_environment: Design studio or home office; creative environment with computer-based design, client meetings, material showroom visits, and site supervision during construction. Mix of conceptual creativity and practical project management.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can establish own interior design studio.

careers.title: Marine Biologist
careers.description: A Marine Biologist studies living organisms in ocean, coastal, and aquatic environments — from microscopic plankton and coral polyps to sharks, dolphins, sea turtles, and deep-sea invertebrates — advancing scientific understanding for conservation, fisheries management, ocean health monitoring, biotechnology (blue economy), and climate change research. India's 7,500 km coastline, 2.02 million sq km EEZ, the Lakshadweep and Andaman coral reef systems, the Sundarbans mangroves, and the deep waters of the Indian Ocean provide extraordinarily rich research environments. Marine Biologists in India work at NIO (National Institute of Oceanography) Goa, CMFRI Kochi, NCAOR Goa, CMLRE Kochi, and university marine science departments. This is related to but distinct from Marine Conservationist (ID 343) in research focus vs conservation practice.
personality_traits: Deep knowledge of marine taxonomy, ecology, and physiology
Advanced diving and underwater survey skills (PADI Research Diver)
Molecular biology and genetics techniques for marine applications
Statistical analysis of ecological datasets
Scientific writing and publication skills
Grant writing for DST, DBT, and international science funding
Field research design for ocean and coastal environments
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Science (Physics, Chemistry, Biology — Biology essential; Marine Biology ideal)
entrance_exams: CUET (for B.Sc Marine Biology / Zoology)
GATE (Ecology and Evolution — EY or Life Sciences — XL for PhD pathway)
CSIR-NET (Life Sciences — for research fellowship)
DBT-JRF (for biotechnology marine research)
Goa University / Pondicherry University / CUSAT direct entry exams for marine science
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in Science (PCB; Biology and Chemistry essential).
2. B.Sc Marine Biology or B.Sc Zoology (Marine Biology specialisation).
3. M.Sc Marine Biology or M.Sc Marine Science (Goa University, Pondicherry University, CUSAT Kochi — premier).
4. PADI Open Water + Advanced SCUBA certification — essential for fieldwork.
5. CSIR-NET/JRF or DBT-JRF for funded PhD at NIO, CMFRI, ICAR, or central universities.
6. Entry: Research Project Assistant at NIO, CMFRI, or NCAOR.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: B.Sc Marine Biology (Govt): INR 15,000-80,000/yr; M.Sc Marine Biology (Govt): INR 15,000-1,00,000/yr; PhD (fellowship-funded): free + INR 37,000-54,000/month; PADI certifications: INR 25,000-60,000; Field research equipment (shared at institutes): institute-provided
careers.career_growth_path: Research Project Assistant → Junior Scientist (Marine) → Marine Biologist → Senior Scientist → Principal Scientist → Director of Marine Research → Vice Chancellor (Marine University)

work_environment.places_of_work: NIO (National Institute of Oceanography) Goa — CSIR, CMFRI (Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute) Kochi — ICAR, NCAOR (National Centre for Antarctic and Ocean Research) Goa, CMLRE (Centre for Marine Living Resources and Ecology) Kochi, Wildlife Institute of India (Marine Division), Madras University, Goa University, Anna University — coastal marine science departments.
work_environment.work_environment: Research laboratory and open-ocean/coastal fieldwork; boat surveys, diving expeditions, beach transects, and ship-based oceanographic cruises (NIO research vessels). Seasonal fieldwork. Highly rewarding for ocean-passionate scientists. Government research posts are stable with 7th Pay Commission salaries.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own marine biotechnology startup or eco-tourism advisory firm.

careers.title: Wellness Coach / Health Coach
careers.description: A Wellness Coach or Health Coach partners with individuals to help them achieve sustainable improvements in health behaviours — including physical activity, nutrition habits, stress management, sleep quality, smoking cessation, and work-life balance — through goal-setting, motivational interviewing, and accountability. Unlike a Nutritionist (who focuses on diet) or a Personal Trainer (who focuses on exercise), a Wellness Coach addresses the whole person and all pillars of health. India's corporate wellness market is growing rapidly (INR 3,500+ crore, KPMG India 2024) as companies invest in employee health to reduce absenteeism and improve productivity. Wellness Coaches work at corporate wellness companies (Mindhouse, HumFit, Practo Wellness), hospitals (preventive health programmes), resorts (Ananda, Shreyas, SwaSwara), and independent practice.
personality_traits: Motivational interviewing and behaviour change methodology
Understanding of all wellness dimensions (physical, mental, social, spiritual)
Goal-setting frameworks (SMART, appreciative inquiry)
Active listening and coaching presence
Knowledge of preventive health metrics (BMI, metabolic health markers)
Programme design for group and individual wellness
Boundary clarity between coaching and clinical care
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; no formal stream requirement; ICF (International Coaching Federation) or National Board for Health and Wellness Coaching (NBHWC) certification is the professional standard
entrance_exams: No formal national entrance exam
No dedicated national wellness coach exam; ICF ACC/PCC and NBHWC-HWC are internationally respected certifications
AYUSH Wellness Centre government employment requires specific AYUSH qualifications
Yoga and wellness degrees (BNYS) at naturopathy colleges are relevant for holistic practitioners
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. Graduate in any discipline (Health Science, Psychology, or any field).
3. ICF-accredited coaching training programme (100 hours minimum for ACC credential): Symbiosis Coaching, The Coaching Academy India, Marshall Goldsmith Stakeholder Centered Coaching.
4. National Board for Health and Wellness Coaching (NBHWC) HWC examination — most rigorous wellness coaching certification.
5. Build 100 hours of coaching practice (required for ICF ACC credential).
6. Entry: Corporate Wellness Coach at Mindhouse, HumFit, Practo Wellness, or establish independent practice.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: Graduation (Govt): INR 20,000-1,00,000; ICF-accredited coaching programme: INR 50,000-3,00,000; NBHWC HWC exam: approx INR 25,000-40,000 (USD 395); ICF ACC credential: approx INR 10,000-15,000; Wellness coaching app subscription: INR 500-2,000/month
careers.career_growth_path: Wellness Coach Trainee → Associate Wellness Coach → Wellness Coach → Senior Wellness Coach → Head of Wellness → Chief Wellness Officer / Wellness Programme Founder

work_environment.places_of_work: Corporate wellness companies (Mindhouse, HumFit, Practo Wellness, Mfine, YourDOST, Fitterfly), luxury wellness resorts (Ananda in the Himalayas Rishikesh, Shreyas Retreat Bengaluru, SwaSwara Gokarna, Vana Malsi Estate Dehradun), hospital preventive health programmes (Apollo Prevention, Fortis La Femme), insurance company wellness programmes (HDFC ERGO wellness, Niva Bupa health coaching), independent practice.
work_environment.work_environment: Corporate office, wellness centre, resort, or home-based (video coaching). Mix of one-on-one coaching sessions, group workshops, and programme administration. Flexible scheduling. High autonomy for independent practitioners.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Can start own wellness coaching practice or corporate wellness company.

careers.example_from_field: Rohini Bajekal — Board-certified Health and Wellness Coach (NBHWC), registered nutritionist, and Director of Education at Plant-Based Health Professionals UK; India-trained physician; her evidence-based lifestyle medicine approach — combining nutrition science, behaviour change, and wellness coaching — has built a global following; one of India's most internationally recognised wellness professionals.

careers.title: Film and TV Producer
careers.description: A Film and TV Producer manages the business, creative, and logistical aspects of bringing a film, web series, television show, or documentary to life — from identifying and developing stories, securing financing and co-production deals, hiring the director and key crew, managing production budgets, navigating censorship (CBFC) and distribution, to delivering the final product to OTT platforms, theatres, or broadcasters. India's entertainment industry is one of the world's largest: Bollywood produces 1,500+ films annually (across all languages), and the OTT market (Netflix India, Amazon Prime Video India, Disney+ Hotstar, Sony Liv, Zee5 — 60+ crore OTT subscribers) has created unprecedented demand for original content (web series, films, documentaries). India's film industry generates INR 20,000+ crore annually.
personality_traits: Story evaluation and creative development judgement
Film financing and co-production deal structuring
Budget management for film and TV productions
Understanding of CBFC certification and broadcasting regulations
Distribution strategy (OTT, theatrical, satellite)
Talent and crew negotiation and management
Risk management across long production cycles
careers.required_stream_10_plus_2: Any stream; Mass Communication, MBA, or Film School background is most relevant; no formal stream restriction
entrance_exams: FTII entrance exam (for Producing programme at FTII Pune)
Satyajit Ray Film and Television Institute entrance
IIMC entrance (for television production pathway)
No dedicated national Film Producer exam; FTII diploma and media industry experience are the primary pathways
Producers Guild of India membership is professional recognition
careers.educational_pathway:
1. Complete 10+2 in any stream.
2. BA Mass Communication or any degree.
3. FTII Pune — TV and Film Production Programme (India's premier) or Whistling Woods Film School.
4. Alternatively: begin as a production assistant, assistant director, or line producer — learn on the job.
5. Study film finance: NFDC (National Film Development Corporation) co-production schemes, soft money (state subsidies, NFDC loans), presales, gap financing.
6. Join the Producers Guild of India — for networking and industry knowledge.
7. Entry: Associate Producer or Line Producer → Producer for a short film or micro-budget project.
Please check the duration of the course during enrolment.
careers.course_fee_inr: BA Mass Communication (Govt): INR 20,000-1,00,000/yr; FTII Production Diploma (Govt-subsidised): INR 20,000-70,000/yr; Whistling Woods production programme: INR 2,00,000-8,00,000; NFDC script development grant: INR 3,00,000-10,00,000; Short film production budget (first project): INR 2,00,000-20,00,000
careers.career_growth_path: Production Assistant → Line Producer → Associate Producer → Producer → Senior Producer → Executive Producer → Studio Head / Production Company Founder

work_environment.places_of_work: Bollywood production houses (Dharma Productions, Excel Entertainment, Red Chillies Entertainment, T-Series Films, Yash Raj Films, Maddock Films), OTT content studios (Netflix India Originals, Amazon Prime Video India, Hotstar Specials, Sony Liv Originals), independent production companies, television production companies (Balaji Telefilms, Star Network Productions, Sony Entertainment), documentary film companies, NFDC-supported art cinema.
work_environment.work_environment: Production office and set; highly variable — planning phases are office-intensive; shooting is location-based with long hours; post-production involves studio work. High pressure during shooting schedules and release windows. Extremely varied income.
work_environment.entrepreneurship_opportunity: Film and TV production IS entrepreneurship — most producers found own companies.

careers.example_from_field: Ritesh Sidhwani — Co-Founder of Excel Entertainment with Farhan Akhtar; one of India's most successful independent film producers; produced Dil Chahta Hai (2001), Zindagi Na Milegi Dobara (2011), Gully Boy (2019), and the Mirzapur web series on Amazon Prime; built Excel Entertainment from a small production house into India's most critically acclaimed independent studio; a role model for entrepreneurial film producing in India.
